# ise_network_access_dictionary_attribute (Resource)

This resource can manage a Network Access Dictionary Attribute.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ise_network_access_dictionary_attribute" "example" {
  dictionary_name = "Opnet"
  name            = "Network_Physics-Attribute"
  description     = "Network Physics Attribute for ARX appliances"
  data_type       = "STRING"
  direction_type  = "BOTH"
  internal_name   = "Network_Physics-Attribute"
  allowed_values = [
    {
      key   = "key1"
      value = "value1"
    }
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `data_type` (String) The data type for the dictionary attribute
  - Choices: `BOOLEAN`, `DATE`, `FLOAT`, `INT`, `IP`, `IPv4`, `IPv6`, `IPV6PREFIX`, `LONG`, `OCTET_STRING`, `STRING`, `UNIT32`, `UNIT64`
- `dictionary_name` (String) The name of the dictionary the attribute belongs to
- `name` (String) The dictionary attribute name

### Optional

- `allowed_values` (Attributes List) List of allowed values for the attribute (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--allowed_values))
- `description` (String) The description of the dictionary attribute
- `direction_type` (String) The direction type for the dictionary attribute
  - Choices: `BOTH`, `IN`, `NONE`, `OUT`
- `internal_name` (String) The internal name of the dictionary attribute

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The id of the object

<a id="nestedatt--allowed_values"></a>
### Nested Schema for `allowed_values`

Optional:

- `key` (String) The allowed value key
- `value` (String) The allowed value

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import ise_network_access_dictionary_attribute.example "Network_Physics-Attribute"
```
